channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Publishes Map Claiming Armed Forces Control Over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran has published a map claiming "armed forces oversight" across more than 22,000 sq km of the strategic waterway, stepping up its assertion of control as tensions with the US and Gulf states continue.

item.title: ✈️ Air France and Airbus Found Guilty of Manslaughter Over 2009 Plane Crash
item.description: A French court found both companies guilty over the Air France Flight 447 disaster that killed 228 people when an Airbus A330 crashed into the Atlantic Ocean en route from Rio to Paris.

item.title: 🇨🇺 US Designates Cuba as National Security Threat as Rubio Accuses Castro Regime
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio declared Cuba a national security threat, with Cuba's foreign minister accusing the US of trying to "instigate a military aggression" amid heightened tensions following the indictment of Raúl Castro.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Trump to Speak With Taiwan Leader About Arms Deal, Risking China Tensions
item.description: President Trump said he would talk to Taiwan's president about a possible weapons sale to the self-governing democracy. Direct dialogue would anger China, which claims Taiwan as its territory and opposes contact between Taiwanese and foreign leaders.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Inside Iran's Revolutionary Guards: The Hard-Line Military Circle Running the Country
item.description: Decision making in Iran is now guided by a small group of men associated with the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps, as the country navigates its confrontation with the US and regional adversaries.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Opens Independent NATO Mission as Regional Tensions Rise
item.description: Japan formally inaugurated an independent mission to NATO, separating from its Belgian embassy, as Tokyo deepens cooperation with the alliance amid escalating tensions with Russia, China, and North Korea.

item.title: 🇪🇺 European Parliament Votes Overwhelmingly for Tougher Iran Sanctions
item.description: The European Parliament adopted a resolution with 516-14 votes demanding expanded EU sanctions on Iranian officials and entities, including the IRGC, over human rights violations and use of the death penalty to suppress dissent.

item.title: ✈️ Singapore Airlines Expands Europe Routes to Capture Gulf Carrier Market Share
item.description: Singapore Airlines is adding long-haul flights to Europe to capture premium traffic diverted from Gulf carriers amid Middle East conflict disruptions, leveraging its strong balance sheet and oil hedging strategy.

item.title: ⚔️ US Navy Accelerates Laser Weapons Development for Future Missile Defense
item.description: Chief of Naval Operations Adm. Daryl Caudle testified that directed energy weapons are critical for naval warfare, arguing lasers could free up VLS cells currently used for defensive interceptors for offensive weapons.

item.title: ☢️ Russia Launches Nuclear War Games on Belarus Border
item.description: Russia conducts major nuclear military exercises in Belarus as NATO issues stark warnings. The drills are seen as an attempt to regain military initiative by threatening escalation on Europe's doorstep.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Euro Zone Growth to Slow in 2026 as Middle East Conflict Fuels Inflation
item.description: The European Commission forecasts economic slowdown in 2026 as the Middle East conflict triggers the second energy shock in five years. Impact severity depends on conflict duration.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Auto Exports to Middle East Nearly Wiped Out by War
item.description: Japanese vehicle exports to the Middle East collapsed in April as the US-Israeli war on Iran disrupted shipping routes critical for automakers like Toyota and Nissan.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Diplomacy Unraveling as Allies Forge New Channels
item.description: Investigation reveals how Trump's threats, personal envoys and hollowed-out embassies are reshaping US diplomacy. Allies across Europe and Asia are ignoring presidential rhetoric and creating alternative diplomatic pathways.

item.title: 🇧🇷 Brazil Senator Bolsonaro Seeks White House Meeting Amid Campaign Crisis
item.description: Senator Flavio Bolsonaro seeks meeting with President Trump as presidential hopeful attempts to distance himself from scandal-hit banker revelations.

item.title: 🇻🇪 Exxon Negotiates Return to Venezuela in Major Policy Reversal
item.description: US oil giant Exxon is in talks to resume operations in Venezuela, ending a years-long standoff and marking a significant shift in energy diplomacy under the Trump administration.

item.title: 🛢️ IEA Chief Warns Oil Markets Nearing Red Zone as Iran Crisis Continues
item.description: International Energy Agency head warns that ongoing tensions with Iran are pushing global oil markets toward critical levels, with potential supply disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 🇨🇺 Russia and China Condemn US Over Raul Castro Indictment
item.description: Moscow and Beijing criticize Washington decision to charge former Cuban leader Raul Castro with conspiracy to kill US nationals, calling it a pretext for escalation against Cuba.

item.title: ⚖️ Top UN Court Rules Workers Right to Strike Protected
item.description: International Court of Justice issues nonbinding ruling that workers right to strike is protected under key treaty, expected to influence global labor relations.

item.title: ✈️ Chinese Firm Breaks Ground on $5B Aviation Complex in UAE Amid Iran War
item.description: A Chinese state-owned company has launched construction of a massive aviation complex in the UAE, signaling continued economic confidence in the region despite ongoing conflict risks.

item.title: 🚢 Chinese Container Ship Among Few Vessels Crossing Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A Chinese-operated container ship was one of the few vessels to cross the Strait of Hormuz in the past 24 hours, as the critical waterway remains largely blocked amid the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇵🇸 U.S. Threatens to Revoke Palestinian UN Delegation Visas
item.description: The Trump administration warned it would revoke visas for the Palestinian UN delegation if the ambassador pursues candidacy for the UN General Assembly vice presidency. The threat reflects escalating U.S. pressure on Palestinian diplomatic representation.

item.title: 🇨🇺 GAESA: The Military Conglomerate at Center of U.S.-Cuba Tensions
item.description: A Reuters explainer on GAESA, the Cuban military-controlled business empire that has become a focal point in U.S.-Cuba tensions as Secretary Rubio blames the conglomerate for Cuba’s economic crisis.

item.title: ⚖️ Two Men Charged Under New Federal Deepfake Law
item.description: Federal prosecutors charged two men with using AI to create nude videos and photos under a new law targeting deepfake content. This marks one of the first prosecutions under the "Take It Down" legislation.

item.title: 🇨🇺⚠️ Rubio Doubtful on Cuba Diplomacy as Trump Raises Military Threat
item.description: Secretary Rubio expressed skepticism about diplomatic engagement with Cuba while President Trump again raised the possibility of U.S. military intervention, following criminal charges against former Cuban leader Raúl Castro.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Supreme Leader orders uranium to remain in country
item.description: Ayatollah Khamenei issued a directive that Iran's near-weapons-grade uranium must not be sent abroad, hardening Tehran's stance against a key U.S. demand in peace talks.
